title: Constancea nevinii
intro: Constancea nevinii, also known as Nevin's woolly marigold or woolly marigold, is a perennial plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, wounds, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Constancea nevinii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Constancea nevinii is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often employed to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Constancea nevinii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It belongs to the genus Constancea, which is a small group of plants native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The species is named after the American botanist Lester W. The plant is relatively rare and is often found in areas with limited vegetation.
